content.full_text:
Farn PolrrrcAt- Pnecrrces C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-s660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 29,2002
Larry Broedow
Urban Advisors, Inc.
10681 Foothill Blvd., Suite 340
Rancho Cucamonga, CA 91730
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.I-02-206
Dear Mr. Broedow:
This letter is in response to your request for assistance under the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").l Because your letter does not
seek advice regarding a specific govemmental decision, we are treating your request as
one for informal assistance.2
QUESTTON
If you accept a position with an Assembly member's field office, would that
position conflict with your intent to stay employed with a private consulting firm?
CONCLUSION
If you accept this position, you could not make or participate in making decisions
affecting your private sector employer. Additionally, you could not use your position
with the Assembly member's office to influence afiy decision or matter before the State
Allocation Board, or the Office of Public School Construction. See discussion below.
It is also suggested that you contact Assembly Ethics at (916) 319-3752 to see if
there is an internal policy goveming this conduct.
I Government Code sections 8 1 000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2lnformal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice (Government Code $ 831 14; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. g 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)


File No. I-02-206
Page No. 2
FACTS
You request clarification regarding apart-time employment position as a field
representative for a member of the Assembly. You are considering applying for this job
and want to make sure it would not present a conflict of interest with your present
employment.
You currently work for a consulting firm, which assists public school districts in
obtaining funding through the State Allocation Board ("SAB") and Office of Public
School Construction ("OPSC") processes. Your assistance generally involves clarifying
regulations and policy, and assisting the district with filling out and submitting the
necessary forms and applications.
You would like to maintain your position with the consulting firm and would also
like to obtain the position with the Assembly district office.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act provides that no public official3 at any level of state or
local govemment shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his
official position to influence a govemmental decision in which he knows or has reason to
know he has a financial interest.
Section 87103 provides that apublic official has a "financial interest" in a
govefllmental decision "if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
mateial financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on
the official, a member of his or her immediate family," or on any of the official's
economic interests, including, among other thingS, ffiy source of income,
including promised income, which aggregates $500 or more within 12 months
prior to the decision. (g 87103(c); regulation 18703.3.)
Accordingly, if you become employed at the Assembly member's field office,
you would be precluded from making, or participate in making, decisions that could
affect your private sector employer.
Additionally, as apublic official, you are prohibited from using your official
position to influence a goveffImental decision. This would include decisions that are
being made by another governmental agency. (Regulation 18702.3(b).) This would
prevent you from purporting to act as a representative of the Assembly member's staff to
expedite the processing of a matter before the State Allocation Board, the Office of
Public School Construction or any other govemmental agency, on behalf of your private
sector employer.
3 "Public official" is defined as "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
government agency." (Section 82048.) You will become a public official when you begin employment
with the Assembly member's field office.


File No. l-02-206
Page No. 3
This assistance is limited to the provisions of the Political Reform Act. The
Assembly Ethics office may have an internal policy that governs your private sector
employment as well. It is suggested you contact that office at the number provided
above. I have also enclosed a pamphlet entitled,"Cafl I Vote? Conflict of Interest
Overview," which provides a step-by-step conflict-of-interest analysis.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contactme at (866)
275-3772.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
LE.
By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosures
JETjg
